narratives.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion.. and you are still doing
narratives.reasoning: This quote directly criticizes Western (specifically American) foreign policy and perception, portraying it as hypocritical and fear-mongering towards Russia and China without justification.

narratives.evidence_quote: amerikans are the greatest pigs on the planet…and that is in the most derogitive term you can imagine.. absolute pigs
narratives.reasoning: This is a severe and derogatory criticism of the American people, directly attacking their character and morality, which aligns with discrediting the West.

narratives.narrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West
narratives.evidence_quote: thanks mostly to un-elected bureaucrats in all “western countries” they are proving the worst of the worst of humanity
narratives.reasoning: This statement predicts and highlights a negative outcome (proving the worst of humanity) for Western countries, blaming their unelected bureaucrats.

narratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
narratives.reasoning: This quote explicitly questions the reliability and framing of Western media coverage of the war, suggesting it is prolonging the conflict.

narratives.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly
narratives.reasoning: This statement evokes fear and anxiety about the potential for nuclear war, presenting it as an almost desirable outcome due to desperation, which amplifies war-related fears.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: The West is overreacting
subnarratives.evidence_quote: amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion.. and you are still doing
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly accuses the West (specifically America) of consistently and disproportionately exaggerating threats from Russia and China, which aligns with the definition of presenting the West's reaction as disproportionate.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: The West does not care about Ukraine, only about its interests
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death. The question is, why the desperation to keep the war going?
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote suggests the West, through its media, is deliberately prolonging the war in Ukraine for its own reasons (implied interests), disregarding the resulting death and suffering, which fits the subnarrative of the West using Ukraine for its own benefit.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: Diplomacy does/will not work
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Sadly the western media is framing the war as if Russia is losing which only prolongs negotiations and causes more death.
subnarratives.reasoning: The statement implies that diplomatic efforts (negotiations) are being undermined and rendered ineffective by Western media's framing, supporting the subnarrative that discredits the potential of diplomatic efforts.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Discrediting the West, Diplomacy: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: I do not think an amerikan life is worth 1/2 as much as an African (or any other life outside the west) ..they are trash ..not worth the effort ..get to a point of “kill them all” either because they do not care and they want us all dead ..or because they just sit back and “DO NOT CARE”
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote supports the parent narrative of discrediting the West by portraying Western (specifically American) populations as morally bankrupt, uncaring, and deserving of destruction. This extreme dehumanization and condemnation is a clear attack on Western values and people but does not fit any of the more specific diplomatic or strategic subnarratives provided.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West: Sanctions imposed by Western countries will backfire
subnarratives.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion.. and you are still doing …
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote frames Western (specifically American) actions and rhetoric towards Russia as exaggerated and provocative, implying that the West's confrontational stance is based on a false premise and is therefore misguided and likely to lead to negative, self-inflicted consequences.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West: Other
subnarratives.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly and no more of month after month of promises from Trump or threats from the east….POOF..it’s all over …thanks mostly to un-elected bureaucrats in all “western countries” they are proving the worst of the worst of humanity
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote expresses a catastrophic, nihilistic view of the conflict's escalation, directly blaming Western ('un-elected') bureaucrats for pushing the world toward nuclear war. It supports the parent narrative of negative consequences for the West by portraying Western governance as leading to apocalyptic outcomes for its own populations and the world.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Distrust towards Media: Western media is an instrument of propaganda
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Another must listen to interview with information that you are missing from the corporation propaganda western media
subnarratives.reasoning: This quote directly labels Western media as 'corporation propaganda,' framing it as a deceptive instrument that withholds true information, which aligns with the definition of discrediting Western media institutions as propaganda tools.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: for the west it’s always been the soviet (RUSSIAN) that has ALWAYS been the boogie man..when in fact they were SELDOM a threat.. amerika always blew any comment about RUSSIA or at later stages CHINA way out of proportion.. and you are still doing
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement supports the parent narrative of distrust towards media by implying that Western narratives (presumably propagated by media) consistently and falsely exaggerate threats from Russia and China, creating a 'boogie man.' This is a general distrust of Western information channels but does not specifically target media institutions as instruments of propaganda or focus on Ukrainian media.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Amplifying war-related fears: There is a real possibility that nuclear weapons will be employed
subnarratives.evidence_quote: Will they resort to nuclear weapons to even out the war?
subnarratives.reasoning: This direct question explicitly raises the possibility of nuclear weapon use in the context of the Ukraine war, evoking fear about such an escalation.

subnarratives.subnarrative_name: URW: Amplifying war-related fears: By continuing the war we risk WWIII
subnarratives.evidence_quote: lets get on with a full nuke war…wipe us all out …at least it (for most) will be over quickly
subnarratives.reasoning: This sarcastic and despairing statement frames the continuation of the current conflict as inevitably leading to a full-scale nuclear war that would annihilate everyone, directly amplifying fears of WWIII.

subnarratives.evidence_quote: I am sure Scott will have a safe place he can go .. he talks the most sense ….pity he cannot all but talk about it….most of the rest of the “un Nuclear war countries” will have to survive the horrors left behind
subnarratives.reasoning: This statement supports the parent narrative of amplifying war-related fears by evoking a bleak, apocalyptic vision of the aftermath of a nuclear conflict, focusing on survival and horrors for most of the world. It does not fit the specific definitions of fearing WWIII, Russia attacking others, NATO intervention, or the immediate use of nukes, but it clearly amplifies fear related to the war's potential catastrophic outcome.
